threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it a 'corner shop' instead of a 'local'. Only true Dalstonites know it’s all about the 'vintage thrift' vibe.
Caught a night bus home and the driver switched off the main lights, turning the ride into a disco with just the glowing exit signs. Unforgettable!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I saw a goat munching on someone's garden while a couple argued in perfect harmony over the best döner nearby.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a döner place that stays open till 5 AM, serving up perfect late-night cabbage and mystery sauces while the night owls debate life.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: People from here know to check BART's status before heading to a show. Out-of-towners? They're still figuring out how to navigate those delays on a Friday.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus with a DJ set up in the back, blasting 90s hip-hop. Riders were bobbing their heads like it was a rooftop party.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists act like Gràcia's plazas are all about sunlight and tapas, but locals know they're often just a backdrop for street art and impromptu jam sessions.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by their bewildered expressions at the Mercat de l'Abaceria. They’re the ones still holding onto the map while trying to order a 'café con leche.'

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still check train times on your phone instead of asking the barista, and your eye twitches when someone says 'night tube.'
Saw a bloke on the 196 last night arguing with a lamp post. Kept insisting it knew where to find the best jollof rice. Absolute scenes.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a couple having a picnic at the U-Bahn station while waiting for a bus that never came. That's Neukölln for you.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you stumble upon a techno party in a playground at 2 AM, complete with makeshift bars and glow sticks. Pure Berlin.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they're from here when they casually mention a BART delay like it's the weather, but a 'transit breakdown' just makes them roll their eyes.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a guy playing violin on the 6 bus, then a lady started singing along loudly. Muni turned into a mini concert, only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia's just for partying; they miss the quiet mornings at local bakeries, where the real community brews over coffee.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them easily at the grocery store, still amazed by the number of varieties of olives and asking what to do with them.
